content: Implement a minimal first-day TYF book-start path: create and open a work, create author-owned source/interview prompts, capture provenance-safe source/voice/claim/question notes, never write manuscript content except through tyf write --confirm, and make the repo valid as a Codex plugin for immediate Codex use. {kind=FRe, subject=tyf.first-day-writing-path, value=source-first-codex-plugin, surface=Codex session 2026-06-21}

TYF should be maximally transparent like SOLO: expose its reflexes and hook behavior clearly, and support explicit git recovery snapshots for book-writing workspaces without silently committing or hiding changes from the author. {kind=FRe, subject=tyf.transparent-reflexes-git, value=visible-hooks-explicit-snapshot, surface=Codex session 2026-06-21}

content: {importance=standard} TYF author decisions should support partial manuscript acceptance: an author can accept explicit source line ranges from a proposal, the decision record stores that accepted subset, and the controlled write applies only the accepted ranges while preserving the existing proposal, audit, evidence, source-hash, base-hash, and path-safety checks. {kind=FRe, subject=tyf.partial-acceptance, value=line-range-decisions, surface=TYF Roast v0.2.1 hardening continuation 2026-06-22}

{importance=standard} TYF works should carry explicit writing-language metadata so multilingual authors can start books in any language without English-only assumptions: start, begin, and new-work store the language in work.yaml, first-session packets surface it, and prose conventions are language-aware rather than universally English. {kind=FRe, subject=tyf.multilingual-authorship, value=explicit-work-language, surface=Codex session 2026-06-22 multilingual side-note}

content: {importance=standard} TYF Gate review records should be tamper-evident: proposal, audit, and decision JSON records created by the helper are sealed with their canonical hash, controlled writes refuse records whose current content no longer matches the seal, and doctor reports any missing or mismatched Gate record seal so post-acceptance edits cannot silently change what enters the manuscript. {kind=FRe, subject=tyf.gate-record-integrity, value=sealed-review-records, surface=TYF Roast v0.2.1 hardening continuation 2026-06-22 record lineage self-attack}

content: {importance=standard} TYF controlled manuscript writes should acquire a per-work manuscript-unit lock before mutating a destination, refuse when an outstanding unit lock exists, release the lock after success or refusal paths that hold it, and have tyf doctor surface any outstanding lock so scheduled or concurrent agents cannot silently race writes into the same unit. {kind=FRe, subject=tyf.concurrent-write-locking, value=manuscript-unit-locks, surface=TYF Roast v0.2.1 hardening continuation 2026-06-22 concurrent write self-attack}
